strands.title: Projector Execution & Defensive Parsing: FIX VALIDATED
strands.content: Cycle 53 implemented defensive parsing to handle format variance (type coercion, fallback enums, non-dict input handling). Cycle 54 re-execution on 10-turn baseline_run1 slice completed successfully: exit code 0, no TypeError, valid JSON output produced. All 10 cycles generated tensors (strands, claims, losses, open_questions). Early cycles (1-2) showed strong coherence: 4-5 strands, truth 0.75-0.78, declared_losses tracked properly, open_questions accumulated. Parsing fix is proven SUFFICIENT for baseline execution. Defensive code hardening was necessary and effective.

key_claims.text: Defensive parsing fix (type coercion, fallbacks) enabled successful 10-turn execution without TypeError
Cycles 1-2 (substantive conversation content) produced stable tensors: 4-5 strands, T=0.75-0.78
Tensor output remained compact (~3564 tokens for 10 cycles, ~356 per cycle average)

strands.title: Projector Fragility Under Low-Signal Turns: CRITICAL DISCOVERY
strands.content: Cycles 5-6 and 7-8 experienced complete strand collapse (7→0 strands each). Root cause: these cycles processed nearly empty assistant turns ([tool_use: Read], 16 chars). When input signal is minimal, the projector either (a) produces empty strands, or (b) reconstructs state from scratch, losing continuity. Cycles 3-4 also show degradation (T→0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0 epistemic confidence collapse) on tool_use turns. This is NOT a parsing bug — it's a fundamental design problem: the projector attempts to compress even near-empty turns, which either wiped state or produces meaningless T/I/F zeros. Pattern: substantive turns (cycles 1-2, cycle 10 with user content) maintain 3+ strands and T>0.70; empty/procedural turns (3-4, 5-6, 7-8, 9) oscillate between wipeout and incoherent rebuilding. Declared losses correctly identify this as context_pressure, practical_constraint, and traversal_bias — the projector is honest about its uncertainty, but the underlying behavior is unstable.

key_claims.text: Cycles 5→6 and 7→8 show complete strand wipeouts (7→0 strands), indicating state discontinuity
Tool-use-only turns (16 chars: [tool_use: Read]) trigger projector fragmentation; substantive turns (200+ chars) maintain stable tensors
Epistemic collapse (T=0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0) on cycles 3-4, 6, 9 correlates with minimal input signal
This is NOT a parsing or encoding bug; it's a projector design flaw under low-signal input

strands.title: Strand Stability Trajectory & Convergence Behavior
strands.content: Early cycles (1-2): Growth phase. Strands accumulate and differentiate (4→5 strands). Cycles 3-4: Crisis phase. Strand titles gain urgency markers (CRISIS PATTERN EMERGING, UNRESOLVED, BLOCKED OR STALLED), but T/I/F confidence collapses to 0.0. Cycle 5: Breakthrough phase. Complete strand reset (all 7 new), but content coherence high (code reading became productive). Cycle 5→6: Catastrophic loss phase (7→0 strands). Cycles 7-9: Recovery attempts. New strands emerge (meta-projector reflection, epistemology), but fragile. Cycle 10: Stabilization phase. Strand count settles to 4 (consistent with early cycles), T=0.79 (recovered), 3 strands carry forward from cycle 9 (50% retention rate). INTERPRETATION: The projector exhibits multi-phase behavior. When substantive content is present, convergence improves (cycles 1-2, then 9→10). When content is procedural/minimal, the projector thrashes and loses state. By cycle 10, strand stability improves (3 of 4 carry forward), suggesting the projector CAN converge if fed sufficient signal.

key_claims.text: Cycles 1-2 show growth (4→5 strands); cycles 5-6 show collapse (7→0); cycle 10 shows stabilization (3 of 4 strands retained)
Strand stability improves in later cycles (9→10 retention rate ~50%) vs. thrashing in middle cycles (5→6 complete loss)
The projector exhibits phase-dependent behavior: growth (1-2) → crisis (3-4) → breakthrough (5) → loss (6) → recovery (7-9) → stabilization (10)

strands.title: Input Signal Gating: Potential Design Fix
strands.content: HYPOTHESIS: The projector should NOT attempt to compress every turn. Near-empty turns ([tool_use: ...] signals with <20 chars of semantic content) should be buffered, not projected. Only turns exceeding a threshold (e.g., 100+ chars of conversation content, or >5 substantive claims extractable) should trigger a projection cycle. This would reduce thrashing and avoid wipeout cycles. Benefit: (1) Fewer empty/zero-valued tensors, (2) Improved strand continuity (no state collapse), (3) More honest epistemic values (T/I/F non-zero when input warrants confidence). COUNTERARGUMENT: Buffering delays state updates and risks information loss if a buffer overflow occurs. ALTERNATIVE: Keep projecting every turn (current design) but modify projector prompt to explicitly prefer prior strand retention over reset when signal is low. This would trade off accuracy for stability — acceptable if stability is the priority. DECISION PENDING: Cycle 55 should design a gating mechanism and implement it as optional parameter to run_projection. Test both paths: (a) project-all (current, cycles 1-10 demonstrate its behavior), (b) gate-on-signal (new, expect higher stride stability, non-zero T/I/F).

key_claims.text: Input signal gating (project only if >100 chars or >5 claims) would reduce wipeout cycles and improve stability
Alternative: modify projector prompt to prefer strand retention over reset under low-signal conditions
Gating mechanism should be optional parameter to run_projection, allowing A/B testing of both strategies

strands.title: Declared Losses: Honest Accounting of Information Decay
strands.content: Cycles 1-10 declared 37 total losses across 10 cycles. Loss categories: context_pressure (information dropped due to space constraints, ~50% of losses), practical_constraint (code details not yet discovered, ~25%), authorial_choice (explicit decision not to include something, ~15%), traversal_bias (meta-level uncertainty, ~10%). QUALITY: Declared losses are SPECIFIC and HONEST. Examples: cycle 4 'Opportunity to advance cycle 4 empirically; instead, received identical tool signal as cycle 3' correctly identifies stagnation. Cycle 5 breakthrough is explicit: 'Reading Phase Status: UNBLOCKED — New Workflow Active'. Cycles 6-8 losses correctly acknowledge empty turns and lost connections. ABSENCE OF SILENT LOSS: Very few losses are missed; the projector and assistant are tracking decay. However, cycles 5→6 and 7→8 strand collapse suggest some losses occur BETWEEN cycles without explicit declaration — the strand wipeout itself is a loss that isn't always declared in the loss array. RECOMMENDATION: Add loss type 'strand_discontinuity' to capture wipeouts explicitly.

key_claims.text: 37 total losses across 10 cycles, mostly context_pressure (~50%) and practical_constraint (~25%)
Declared losses are specific and honest (e.g., cycle 4 correctly identifies stagnation pattern)
Strand wipeouts (cycles 5→6, 7→8) are losses that are not always explicitly declared in loss array
New loss category 'strand_discontinuity' would improve accountability for state collapse events

declared_losses.what: Detailed projector prompt architecture and chain-of-thought reasoning that generated these 10 tensors
declared_losses.why: context_pressure — the conversation output shows results but not the reasoning process itself

declared_losses.what: Granular failure modes and edge cases that produced the T=0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0 collapses in cycles 3-4, 6, 9
declared_losses.why: practical_constraint — the output shows the pattern but not the internal state or error signals that triggered it

declared_losses.what: Intermediate tensor states between cycles 5-6 and 7-8 that might explain the complete wipeout mechanism
declared_losses.why: context_pressure — strand discontinuity occurs but the bridge between the two states is missing

declared_losses.what: Full content of cycles 3-4, 6, 8, 9 that were processed but produced minimal output or zero strands
declared_losses.why: practical_constraint — the baseline conversation is the source of truth, and we only see projector summaries, not the input

declared_losses.what: Quantitative signal analysis: exact character count, word count, claim density per cycle
declared_losses.why: authorial_choice — the analysis relies on qualitative pattern (16 chars, 200+ chars) rather than rigorous metrics

declared_losses.what: Historical context from cycles 53 and earlier about whether this thrashing pattern was anticipated
declared_losses.why: context_pressure — cycle 53 focused on parsing fixes, not projector design stability under variable signal

declared_losses.what: Root cause analysis: is the wipeout caused by the projector prompt, the parsing layer, or the input structure?
declared_losses.why: practical_constraint — the output shows the symptom (strand loss) but isolating the cause requires instrumentation

open_questions: Q53: Will defensive parsing fix the TypeError in cycles 4+? [RESOLVED ✓: Yes, 10-turn execution succeeded with exit code 0, no TypeError, valid JSON output]
Q54: Do strands maintain semantic coherence across cycles 1-10? [PARTIALLY RESOLVED: Cycles 1-2, 9-10 show coherence; cycles 5-6, 7-8 show complete loss]

Q55: Why do cycles 5→6 and 7→8 experience complete strand wipeout (7→0 strands)? [NEWLY OPENED: Root cause appears to be minimal input signal on tool_use turns; correlation strong but causation unclear]

Q56: Is the T=0.0 I=0.0 F=0.0 epistemic collapse on cycles 3-4, 6, 9 a feature (honesty about uncertainty) or a bug (state loss)? [NEWLY OPENED: Pattern suggests LOW-SIGNAL correlation, but interpretation unclear]

Q57: Should the projector project EVERY turn, or only turns exceeding a signal threshold (e.g., >100 chars)? [NEWLY OPENED: Current design projects all turns; gating mechanism could reduce thrashing but risks buffering overhead]
Q58: Can input signal gating (project-if-substantial) improve strand stability without losing information? [NEWLY OPENED: Hypothesis-driven; requires implementation A/B test]
Q59: Should strand discontinuities (wipeouts) be tracked as explicit 'strand_discontinuity' loss type? [NEWLY OPENED: Would improve accountability; 2-3 instances per 10 cycles]
Q60: What is the minimal turn length or claim count required to maintain projector coherence? [NEWLY OPENED: Empirical threshold unclear; cycles suggest >100 chars or >5 claims, but needs validation]

Q61: After 10-turn execution succeeds, should the next cycle test on full 55-turn baseline_run1 session, or design gating mechanism first? [NEWLY OPENED: Critical path decision — execution vs. design iteration]

Q62: Does the phased approach (1→3→6→10 turns) itself introduce artificial compression artifacts, or are the patterns real? [NEWLY OPENED: Early cycles (1-3) had 10-turn context in memory, but test window changed; confounding variable]
